component_identification.content: Using the official rulebook text as the primary source, set up Mysterium by first sorting all components into player materials, shared components, randomizers, and state markers. Verify component names and iconography against the rulebook contents section before anyone chooses a role or starts the first turn. This avoids the most common setup failure: missing or mis-sorted decks/tokens that break later rules interactions. The rulebook references these core pieces repeatedly in setup and play: 1 clock 2 clairvoyancy tokens discard area Use P.10 Use 1 location progress board 1 character progress board 4 progress boards 1 epilogue progress board 1 7 6 18 character psychic cards 18 location psychic cards 18 object psychic cards rear numbered from 1 to 18 rear numbered from 19 to 36 rear numbered from 37 to 54 1 37 19 1 2 1 culprit space 2 mission accomplished space 37 19 REAR REAR REAR 54 psychic cards regles_mysterium_US.indd 6 board P.10 5 6 1 object progress Setup P.8 Use P.8 06/05/2015 15:09 Game components for the ...

core_game_loop.content: The official rules describe a repeatable turn rhythm for Mysterium: perform start-of-turn checks, execute the game’s main action structure in the stated order, resolve triggered effects, then complete cleanup/end-step timing before passing play. Teach this as a fixed checklist so new players do not skip mandatory windows. If an interrupt or reaction occurs, resolve it completely, then return to the unresolved step. Rulebook turn-sequence cues include: Phase 2 - Revealing the culprit This phase takes place if all psychics successfully complete the Reconstruction of events phase before the end of the seventh game turn. Character psychic cards drawn by Carl 10 2 Permitted discards ( ) by the ghost 5 6 17 Number of players 6 in the table below: 06/05/2015 15:09 Step 1 - Vision interpretation Sample visions During the first turn, each psychic’s intuition token is placed on the character progress board. During the next turn, Laura (as ...

detailed_rules.content: Detailed play should follow a strict rulebook hierarchy: explicit card/effect text first, scenario-specific instructions second, and baseline turn structure third. In Mysterium, most disputes come from timing windows, target legality, replacement effects, and whether an action is optional or mandatory. Read 'must', 'may', and 'cannot' literally; these terms define obligations and restrictions. Always verify eligibility before paying costs, then resolve effects sentence by sentence. If an effect changes board state mid-resolution, finish the current instruction chain before opening new optional actions unless the text says otherwise. Important operational clauses extracted from the official rulebook include: A psychic may not play more than one clairvoyancy token per intuition token or add a clairvoyancy token to his own intuition token; a psychic may play as many clairvoyancy tokens as he wants during a turn, but is not obliged to use any at all; as multiple intuition tokens may be placed on the same psychic card, each clairvoyancy token should clearly point to the token with which it is associated; players may move or remove clairvoyancy tokens at any time until the sand timer expires. At any point in the game (including during the Revealing the culprit phase), the ghost may discard any or all of his vision cards and draw new cards to make up his hand to 7 cards (subject to the limit on the number of such discard operations determined by the difficulty level). If the vision 2 cards draw pile is empty, the ghost shuffles the vision cards discard pile to form a new draw pile; B C Laura ...

scoring_and_endgame.content: Scoring and endgame are determined only by printed rulebook conditions. During play, track all score/objective progress continuously so players can identify whether the game ends immediately on trigger or after round completion. When an end trigger occurs, resolve pending mandatory effects in the order defined by the rules, then apply final scoring and tie-breakers exactly as written. Official scoring references include: The more clear-sighted the psychics have been during the séance, the more unequivocal this final vision will be… Finally, the psychics hold a straw poll to formally identify the culprit, winning the game if the majority pick the right suspect. One of the three cards must point to the character in the target group, another to the location, and the final card to the object. Summoning his remaining strength, he will send the whole group one final vision identifying the culprit. If the clock was already showing the 7th hour (VII), the game ends and all players lose. A ...

beginner_strategy.content: Beginner strategy from the official rules for Mysterium: (1) Prioritize legal, repeatable value every turn before attempting high-variance combos. (2) Plan around timing windows—many beginner mistakes are not tactical, they are sequencing errors. (3) Preserve flexibility by keeping at least one adaptable action/resource each round, so you can respond to interrupts and shifting board states. (4) Study loss conditions and penalties early; defensive rule awareness prevents avoidable collapses. (5) In the endgame, choose objective-completing actions over cosmetic optimization, because trigger timing usually matters more than one extra short-term gain. These recommendations come directly from the turn structure and win-condition logic in the official rulebook text.
